translated: Yeah, when we first learned the choreo, we all felt like our souls were leaving our bodies.
We were all so worried about whether we could actually sing and dance it at the same time, but the more we practiced, the more our breathing synced up. Eventually, that anxiety turned into pure fun.
So on the day of the live, I felt more of a sense of unity with the audience than worry. How about you guys?
Yeah, definitely.
Right. Ever since "High Motivation" was announced...
...everyone was so excited to see the performance. I felt a lot of pressure, but...
I also thought the choreo would be a bit simpler, or maybe have a "sweeter" feel to it.
I thought so too.
But it's a lot of dancing!
Why is it like that?
I know, right?
I was worried we wouldn't be able to express the fun of the song, but as the live got closer, our practice gave us confidence. We decided to just have fun...
Exactly.
...and show everyone that the 2nd and 3rd gen members can do this! We wanted to deliver that power.
Yeah.
In the end, we had fun, but at first, we couldn't even think about that. We were just desperate. When we first tried singing and dancing together...
Right.
...we were all like, "This is impossible! This is really bad!"
Even though there are fewer of us than the full 11, we couldn't even catch our breath. The formations were so difficult.
And the rhythm isn't just a simple 1-2-3-4 beat.
It's like, "Wait, we're dancing to those tiny little sounds too?" The choreographer really packed it in.
It really was something. But it turned out great!
Yeah, it did.
What part was especially difficult?
From our perspective watching you, like Saku said, you're hitting every single beat.
But since we're just watching, we just end up thinking, "Wow, that looks like fun!"
Right.
So, where was the struggle?
With other songs, I can usually point to one specific part that was hard.
But with this one, every single part was a little bit difficult, so it felt hard the whole time.
I see.
Yeah, that makes sense.
It was just... tough.
How do I put it? Usually, when it's not your turn to sing, you might just be standing there watching the others...
But there's almost none of that in this song.
You're always doing one or the other.
You're constantly moving, and the formations are tough. You just end up totally out of breath.
Yeah.
I get it.
I can say it now, but I was a mess during the choreo lessons too.
Really?
You were?
Everyone was basically crying while doing it.
Seriously.
I was crying my eyes out.
I really wondered what was going to happen.
"What's going to happen to us?"
But I thought, "Mei-chan would never cry..."
Yeah.
...so seeing her cry made me cry even more. I don't even know why.
No, no, that's not true.
Ah.
Well, I can talk about it now.
Everyone practiced so much at home. When we met up again, the quality had improved so much. I was so impressed.
Well, the lyrics even say something about "having changed," right?
Hearing this story makes me think that's exactly what it was about.
I wasn't there since I'm a 1st gen member, but...
Even the title is "High Motivation."
Um...
During rehearsals, the 2nd and 3rd gens would be like, "Alright!"
"High Motivation!" they'd say, pumping themselves up.
Seeing that just made me think, "That's so cool."
Yeah, you really can't get through a song like that without that kind of motivation.
But during the actual performance, everyone looked so happy.
You were all smiling, so I realized how much hard work must have gone into getting to that point.
Everyone worked so hard.
Because everyone gave it their all...
...it turned out great.
By the way, did you see Nako's impression?
Eh? What's that?
You haven't seen it?
You should have her show you sometime.
What is it?
Did you take a video?
No, I didn't, but she's always mimicking the "High Motivation" choreo.
It's so incredibly cute, so you should definitely ask Nako to show you.
I want to see it!
She mimics a very specific part.
Oh, really?
I've gotta see that.
